title: Democratic mayoral candidates at odds over budget cuts
summary: Three Democratic mayoral candidates are divided over a $3.5M budget amendment passed by the Common Council on May 9, 2025. Deputy Mayor Sharon Owens opposes the cuts and criticized the Council for lack of transparency, while Councilors Pat Hogan and Chol Majok—her opponents in the June primary—championed the reductions as fiscal responsibility. The Mayor has 10 days to reject the amendments.

political_signal.description: Budget amendment reflects fiscal conservatism by two mayoral candidates; opponent frames it as abuse of power and lack of due process. Primary election positioning evident.

processing_notes: Dollar amount is $3.5M (not $3 as initially extracted)
